author: Miles Nash
name: EchoHarp + Random Kitchen Sink
blurb: Late-minute non-record research draft: a tiny neural model playing a classic-compressor harp. EchoHarp adds causal copy, recency, and variable-order motif continuation echoes using only input context and five learned scalar strings. A deterministic random kitchen-sink adapter adds a procedural nonlinear lift regenerated from a seed, so only the down-projection, gate, vocabulary prior, and EchoHarp scalars are stored. A small 1xH100 RunPod probe ran end-to-end under the 16MB artifact cap, but slightly trailed the matched baseline.
hardware: RunPod 1x H100 SXM probe, one sp1024 train shard, 50 steps; not an official 8xH100 leaderboard run
technique_summary: Official clean baseline plus EchoHarp causal compressor prior (copy/recency/motif-1/motif-2/motif-3 continuation echoes), RANDOM_FEATURES=192 deterministic ReLU^2 random kitchen-sink adapter, and learned vocabulary logit prior. Frozen random_up buffer is persistent=False and regenerated from RANDOM_FEATURE_SEED=20260430. Tiny matched probe: baseline 2.538749 roundtrip BPB, EchoHarp-only 2.539374, EchoHarp+RFA 2.540498.
